preconditions: When the page loads, the user can see a list of products, each with an image, title, and price.
The shopping cart (designated drop area) is visible and initially empty.
actor_actions: drag a product item from the list by clicking and holding the mouse button on the item.
drop the dragged product item into the designated drop area (shopping cart) by releasing the mouse button over the area.
expected_observables: The cart area displays the product title, quantity '1', and the product price.
The <div id="allMoney"> element displays the product price formatted with the appropriate currency symbol and two decimal places (e.g., '$X.XX').
state_effects: Cart state contains one entry for the product with quantity = 1.
Cart total state equals the product price and is reflected in the UI element #allMoney.
constraints: Products show a predefined title and price in the product list as loaded on page load.

source_evidence.quote: Upon dropping a product into the shopping cart, the system adds the product to the cart and displays the product title, quantity, and price in the cart area.

preconditions: When the page loads, the user can see a list of products, each with an image, title, and price.
The shopping cart (designated drop area) is visible.
actor_actions: drag and drop product A into the shopping cart.
drag and drop product B into the shopping cart.
expected_observables: The cart lists both product A and product B with their prices and quantities.
The <div id="allMoney"> element displays the sum of product A and product B prices formatted with the appropriate currency symbol and two decimal places (e.g., '$80.00').
state_effects: Cart state contains entries for each added product with their respective quantities.
Cart total state equals the arithmetic sum of all product prices and is shown in #allMoney.
constraints: The system formats the displayed total to two decimal places with the appropriate currency symbol.

source_evidence.quote: The system calculates the total price by summing the prices of all products in the cart, formatting the total to two decimal places with the appropriate currency symbol (e.g., '$80.00'), and displays the total in the <div id="allMoney"> element.

preconditions: When the page loads, the user can see a list of products, each with an image, title, and price.
A specific product (Product X) is present in the product list.
actor_actions: drag and drop Product X into the shopping cart multiple times (repeat the drag-drop action).
expected_observables: The cart shows Product X with an incremented quantity reflecting the number of drops.
The <div id="allMoney"> element displays the total equal to (unit price of Product X * quantity) formatted with currency symbol and two decimal places.
state_effects: Cart state increments the quantity for Product X for each drop.
Cart total state increases accordingly and is reflected in #allMoney.
constraints: If the user drops the same product into the cart multiple times, the system increments the quantity of that product in the cart.

source_evidence.quote: If the user drops the same product into the cart multiple times, the system increments the quantity of that product in the cart.

preconditions: When the page loads, the user can see a list of products, each with an image, title, and price.
The shopping cart and the <div id="allMoney"> total element are visible.
actor_actions: perform a sequence of drag-and-drop operations adding products to the cart (one after another).
expected_observables: After each drop action, the <div id="allMoney"> element updates immediately to reflect the new summed total, formatted with the currency symbol and two decimal places.
state_effects: Cart total state is updated incrementally after each add and persisted in the UI element #allMoney.
constraints: The user can see the total cost update in real-time as they add more products to the cart.

source_evidence.quote: The user can see the total cost update in real-time as they add more products to the cart.
